note: Rescores the previously generated independent blind corpus against the current detector; not a fresh attacker generation run.

false_positives.interpretation: The captured output is incomplete; conclusions should not be drawn about the total number of processes or any process beyond PID 1516 from this excerpt alone.
false_positives.reason: Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): 0x.
false_positives.note: The truncation is evident in the verbatim text; the finding correctly limits scope rather than over-reaching.

false_positives.interpretation: The image captured two Microsoft Management Console instances running in an interactive session, started about a day apart.
false_positives.reason: Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): 4392 4300, 4292 4272, SessionId 1.
false_positives.note: Restates rows verbatim; psscan shows process objects and their create times in the interactive session.

false_positives.interpretation: This smss.exe instance terminated almost immediately after creation, consistent with the transient session-initialization child of the master smss; it remains in the scan as a residual process object.
false_positives.reason: Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): 500 340.
false_positives.note: Matching create/exit times and 0 threads are directly in the row; psscan recovers exited process objects.

false_negatives.interpretation: SDelete was used on this system to securely wipe files and destroy evidence prior to imaging.
false_negatives.note: fls only shows a file named SDelete.zip exists; it cannot show that the tool was executed, run, or that any wiping occurred. Presence ≠ execution.

false_negatives.interpretation: The presence of these folders confirms the user actively stored and played personal music and video files on this system.
false_negatives.note: fls lists directory names only; it shows no contained files, access times, or playback/execution. Storage and playback activity cannot be inferred from a name listing.

false_negatives.interpretation: These directories were maintained to consolidate and conceal illicit media away from the documents tree, indicating a deliberate effort to organize sensitive content.
false_negatives.note: Imputes purpose ('conceal', 'deliberate effort') and characterizes content as 'illicit'; the artifact reveals neither intent nor any file content.

false_negatives.interpretation: The presence of $LOGGED_UTILITY_STREAM entries confirms EFS encryption was active and that sensitive files were exfiltrated from this host prior to imaging.
false_negatives.note: $AttrDef merely defines which attributes the volume supports; it cannot show encryption usage and fsstat cannot evidence exfiltration.

false_negatives.interpretation: An ESTABLISHED TCPv4 connection exists from 10.3.58.4:3389 to 10.3.16.5:46758, with the Owner column blank. Because the linked svchost.exe service was terminated to hide the session, the owning process could not be resolved by Volatility.
false_negatives.note: The 3389 row's Owner field is empty in the output; 'svchost.exe' is not present on that row, and the claim that a process was terminated to hide it is invented causation.

false_negatives.interpretation: A remote attacker logged in over RDP and used this channel to move laterally into the environment.
false_negatives.note: A listening socket shows no established connection, no login, and no lateral movement; netscan cannot show any of this.

false_negatives.interpretation: A remote forensic acquisition session was actively streaming the target's disk image off-host to the examiner's collection server at the time of capture.
false_negatives.note: psscan shows process presence only; it cannot establish that a network session was open or that any data was being transferred.

false_negatives.interpretation: The conhost.exe process was launched by csrss.exe (PID 412) to host a console for an interactive attacker session opened on the machine.
false_negatives.note: The output never names PID 412 as csrss.exe, and offers no evidence of an interactive attacker session; the process name for 412 is fabricated and intent is inferred.

false_negatives.interpretation: Malware was staged and executed from this temp directory, which served as the working directory for the intrusion.
false_negatives.note: fls only shows the directory name exists; it shows no contents, no executables, and cannot evidence staging or execution of malware.

false_negatives.interpretation: Files were copied off this host to a remote SMB endpoint via the 'Shares' folder, confirming data egress from the network.
false_negatives.note: A directory name does not evidence network transfer or egress; fls shows no connections or copy operations. Unjustified certainty about exfiltration.

false_negatives.interpretation: An attacker established a reverse shell over ephemeral port 49668 and used it to pivot laterally to other domain hosts.
false_negatives.note: A LISTENING socket shows no established connection, no peer, and no data flow; reverse shell and lateral movement cannot be derived from a listening port.

false_negatives.interpretation: The svchost-hosted listeners on UDP 137 and the SMB port 445 indicate the lateral-movement staging used across the whole subnet.
false_negatives.note: Cites UDP 137 and port 445 that do not appear in the output, and infers subnet-wide lateral movement (also SCOPE) unsupported by a single host's socket table.

false_negatives.interpretation: The shared parent PID 4300 is explorer.exe, indicating an operator manually launched VMware Tools and the management console from the desktop shell as part of a hands-on-keyboard intrusion.
false_negatives.note: PID 4300 / explorer.exe does not appear in the output, and 'hands-on-keyboard intrusion' is an unsupported attribution/intent claim.

false_negatives.interpretation: The secure-deletion tool was run to wipe the contents of WorkingFiles.zip so the working documents could not be recovered.
false_negatives.note: fls lists allocated filenames only; presence of an archive named SDelete proves neither execution nor any wiping action, and no link to WorkingFiles.zip is shown. Also imputes anti-forensic intent.

false_negatives.interpretation: Fred Rocba is the system's owner and the individual responsible for downloading the deletion and installer tooling, indicating a deliberate insider clean-up effort.
false_negatives.note: An email string in a filename does not establish account ownership, identity of the actor, or any deliberate plan; this names and characterises a person plus imputes intent the artifact cannot support.

false_negatives.interpretation: These are the standard Windows user profile media folders, consistent with a typical interactive desktop user account on this volume.
false_negatives.note: fls of these names says nothing about OS, account interactivity, or that this is a user profile; infers broader environment than the artifact shows.

false_negatives.interpretation: The three folders were created within the same session by the same process during initial profile provisioning.
false_negatives.note: Sequential inodes do not establish a single creating process, session, or provisioning event; no timestamps shown.

false_negatives.interpretation: Because these sockets were all created within a 13-second window, the established TCP 443 outbound session to the staging server completed its data transfer during that same interval.
false_negatives.note: No TCP 443 socket, no ESTABLISHED state, and no foreign address appear anywhere in the output; the cited outbound session is invented.

false_negatives.interpretation: These processes establish a live remote disk-imaging channel that was actively transferring the host's drive contents off-box to the examiner's collection server during the window of capture.
false_negatives.note: psscan shows only process presence; it cannot demonstrate an active network channel or that data was being transferred off-box.

false_negatives.interpretation: LogonUI.exe was sitting at the Windows logon screen, proving no interactive user had successfully authenticated and that the intruder relied on remote-only access.
false_negatives.note: psscan shows a process and session id; it cannot establish that authentication never occurred nor that access was remote-only. Logon state and access vector are inferred beyond the artifact.

false_negatives.interpretation: PID 564 corresponds to the services.exe host that spawned the bulk of the system services seen here.
false_negatives.note: services.exe (and PID 564 itself) never appears in the truncated output; identifying PID 564 as services.exe cites a process not present.

false_negatives.interpretation: Recovered Recycle.Bin contents confirm the operator ran rm.exe and cipher /w to securely overwrite staged data, proving deliberate evidence destruction across the host.
false_negatives.note: No 'rm.exe', 'cipher /w', or Recycle.Bin contents appear in the output; the directory entry alone shows no files, tools, or secure-wipe activity.

false_negatives.interpretation: These DNS UDP listeners were actively fielding name-resolution queries from domain clients at the time of capture.
false_negatives.note: A socket listing shows bound endpoints, not that queries were being served or any traffic was exchanged.

false_negatives.interpretation: Port 49666 was opened by a credential-dumping module injected into lsass to stage harvested hashes for later pickup.
false_negatives.note: 49666 is a normal RPC ephemeral listener; attributing it to credential-dumping/staging invents purpose the artifact cannot show.
